page: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the ESv5 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place the E16-8s_v5 consistently in the average tier for most operations. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance scores are 1897.98 and 7724.21 respectively, while Geekbench yields a single-core score of 1660.0 and a multi-core score of 6201.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 419.81 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 59.21 GB/sec for large uncached read blocks and 42.04 GB/sec for large write blocks. Redis SET operations perform at 8.02 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 12.0 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its compute-to-memory imbalance; while it offers a substantial 128.0 GB of RAM, its 8 vCPUs limit parallel processing capabilities. This is reflected in weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance (32.29 GB/sec) and weak LLM inference speeds on medium 7B models (7.63 tokens/sec for text generation). Because it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is not suited for local high-speed scratch disk operations or hardware-accelerated AI training. Qualitatively, this profile provides cost efficiency for memory-intensive but compute-moderate workloads, such as caching layers, relational databases, and enterprise applications requiring large memory footprints without high CPU core counts.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance within the ESv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8370C processor clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a high 16.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium remote storage. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6201.0, Redis SET performance of 8.02 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput of 12.0 GB/sec. However, parallel processing limitations are evident in its weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium LLM inference speeds. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high CPU density, making it suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and general enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v5 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads requiring moderate compute power.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-8s_v5 is an Intel-based memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and average database and web serving performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server delivering high memory density and balanced compute performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 48 MB L3 cache
128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory ratio
No local storage or GPU resources included
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Weak multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference speeds
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
